CheckTitle: Review disabled user accounts still present in the tenant
Description: Identifies user accounts that are disabled (accountEnabled=false) but not yet deleted. Disabled accounts that linger may indicate incomplete offboarding or accumulate stale permissions.
Risk: Disabled accounts left in the directory retain group memberships, role assignments, and app consent grants. If an admin re-enables the account, all previous access is instantly restored. They also inflate licensing counts and create noise in access reviews.

Remediation.Recommendation: Review disabled accounts periodically. Delete accounts for users who have permanently departed. Use Entra ID lifecycle workflows to automate offboarding and account cleanup.

Frameworks.source: AI-generated mapping — not yet verified against published benchmark

SourceNotes: Framework control IDs were AI-generated. This is an informational check — disabled accounts are not inherently a vulnerability but require periodic review.
